claims.claim: This paper reports on a lightweight reproducibility capsule mechanism that captures a directory's full state (file contents, symlink targets, command replay specifications, host metadata, and SHA-256 content hashes) into a JSON capsule, with regex-based redaction of secret-shaped values.
claims.notes: Historical backfill linked the claim to already published artifacts; this is not independent source revalidation.

claims.claim: We validate the mechanism against 80 synthetic messy workflows spanning five variant classes (cache-required, nested paths, paths with spaces, symlinks, and Unicode paths).
claims.notes: Historical backfill linked the claim to already published artifacts; this is not independent source revalidation.

claims.claim: A naive baseline that copies only source-like files failed all 80 replays; capsule replay succeeded in all 80 cases with zero raw-secret leaks.
claims.notes: Historical backfill linked the claim to already published artifacts; this is not independent source revalidation.

claims.claim: Median capture-plus-replay latency was 43.0 ms (p95: 58.6 ms) at a throughput of 9.2 cases/s.

claims.claim: Toy capsules on clean directories may succeed trivially; the mechanism's value depends on whether it handles the messy cases that cause naive handoffs to fail.

claims.claim: This paper reports results from a targeted validation: we implement a stdlib-only capsule prototype, generate 80 synthetic messy workflows across five variant classes, and compare naive baseline replay against capsule replay.
claims.notes: Historical backfill linked the claim to already published artifacts; this is not independent source revalidation.

claims.claim: We measure pass/fail rates, secret leakage, latency, and resource consumption.
